steps.title: Clear fractions to get a quadratic
steps.content: Multiply every term by $156cx$: $$156 = cx^{2} + 156x \implies cx^{2} + 156x - 156 = 0.$$

steps.title: Solve the discriminant equation
steps.content: Exactly one solution means the quadratic's discriminant is $0$. For $cx^2+156x-156=0$, the discriminant is $$156^2-4(c)(-156)=156^2+4c(156).$$ Set the discriminant equal to $0$ and solve to get $c=-39$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Solving the same equation by hand: $$156^2+624c=0$$ $$24{,}336+624c=0$$ $$c=-39.$$ The answer is -39.
